title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, leading to potential data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Immediately audit and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strict access controls, multi-factor authentication, and regular code integrity checks.
Ensure robust backup strategies are in place, with backups stored offline and tested for recovery.
Deploy advanced threat detection and monitoring tools to identify and respond to anomalous activities in cloud environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: OpenTelemetry's disk retry default temp path enables local blob injection via OTLP Exporter
description:
### Summary

The OTLP disk retry feature in `OpenTelemetry.Exporter.OpenTelemetryProtocol` silently fell back to `Path.GetTempPath()` when `OTEL_DOTNET_EXPERIMENTAL_OTLP_RETRY=disk` was set but `OTEL_DOTNET_EXPERIMENTAL_OTLP_DISK_RETRY_DIRECTORY_PATH` was not configured.

The exporter stored and loaded `*.blob` files under fixed, signal-named subdirectories (`traces`, `metrics`, `logs`) beneath that shared temporary root path.

On multi-user systems where the temporary directory is accessible to

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows local users on multi-user systems to inject malicious blob files into the temporary directory used by OpenTelemetry's OTLP disk retry feature. This could lead to unauthorized data manipulation, potential data corruption, or exploitation of downstream systems relying on the tampered data.
recommended_actions: Ensure the `OTEL_DOTNET_EXPERIMENTAL_OTLP_DISK_RETRY_DIRECTORY_PATH` environment variable is explicitly configured to a secure, user-specific directory with restricted permissions.
Apply the latest patches or updates for the OpenTelemetry library to mitigate the vulnerability (CVE-2026-42191).
Restrict access to shared temporary directories on multi-user systems and implement monitoring to detect unauthorized file modifications.

title: A flaw was found in gnutls. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by presenting a...
description: A flaw was found in gnutls. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by presenting a specially crafted Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) response during a TLS handshake. Due to a logic error in how gnutls processes multi-record OCSP responses, a client with OCSP verification enabled may incorrectly accept a revoked server certificate, potentially leading to a compromise of trust.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in gnutls allows a remote attacker to exploit a logic error in OCSP response processing, potentially causing clients to trust revoked server certificates. This could lead to man-in-the-middle attacks or unauthorized access to sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or updates for gnutls as soon as they are available.
Disable OCSP verification temporarily if it is not critical to operations, until a patch is applied.
Monitor network traffic for suspicious TLS handshakes and implement additional certificate validation mechanisms.

title: A flaw was found in gnutls. This vulnerability occurs because gnutls performs case-sensitive...
description: A flaw was found in gnutls. This vulnerability occurs because gnutls performs case-sensitive comparisons of `nameConstraints` labels, specifically for `dNSName` (DNS) or `rfc822Name` (email) constraints within `excludedSubtrees` or `permittedSubtrees`. A remote attacker can exploit this by crafting a leaf certificate with casing differences in the Subject Alternative Name (SAN), leading to a policy bypass where a certificate that should be rejected is instead accepted. This could result in unaut

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in gnutls allows a remote attacker to bypass certificate validation policies by exploiting case-sensitive comparisons in name constraints. This could lead to unauthorized access, data interception, or man-in-the-middle attacks, particularly in systems relying on gnutls for secure communications.
recommended_actions: Update gnutls to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-3833.
Conduct a thorough review of systems and applications relying on gnutls for certificate validation to ensure compliance with updated security policies.
Monitor network traffic for signs of exploitation, such as unauthorized or unexpected certificate usage.

An unauthenticated remote attacker can inject arbitrary shell commands by submitting a POST request with crafted radius_address, radius_address2, shared_secret2, source_ip, timeout, or retry parameters along with save=1 and enable_radius=1

title: Weaver (Fanwei) E-office versions prior to 10.0_20221201 contain an unauthenticated arbitrary...
description: Weaver (Fanwei) E-office versions prior to 10.0_20221201 contain an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload vulnerability in the OfficeServer.php endpoint that allows remote attackers to upload malicious files by sending multipart POST requests with arbitrary filenames and disguised content types. Attackers can upload PHP webshells to the Document directory and execute them via HTTP GET requests to achieve remote code execution as the web server user. Exploitation evidence was first observed by th

recommended_actions: Immediately update Weaver (Fanwei) E-office to version 10.0_20221201 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of server logs to identify any signs of exploitation or unauthorized access.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to monitor and block malicious multipart POST requests targeting the vulnerable endpoint.

If an authenticated administrator visits the malicious webpage, the victim's browser automatically includes the valid ses

title: A flaw in GnuTLS DTLS handshake parsing allows malformed fragments with zero length and non-zero...
description: A flaw in GnuTLS DTLS handshake parsing allows malformed fragments with zero length and non-zero offset, leading to an integer underflow during reassembly and resulting in an out-of-bounds read. This issue is remotely exploitable and may cause information disclosure or denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GnuTLS DTLS handshake parsing can be exploited remotely, potentially leading to information disclosure or denial of service. This poses a significant risk to systems relying on GnuTLS for secure communications.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches or updates provided by the GnuTLS maintainers to address CVE-2026-33845.
Conduct a thorough review of systems and applications using GnuTLS to identify and mitigate potential exposure.
Implement network monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify and respond to potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-41179 - Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage p
description: Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage providers. Starting in version 1.48.0 and prior to version 1.73.5, the RC endpoint `operations/fsinfo` is exposed without `AuthRequired: true` and accepts attacker-controlled `fs` input. Because `rc.GetFs(...)` supports inline backend definitions, an unauthenticated attacker can instantiate an attacker-controlled backend on demand. For the WebDAV backend, `bearer_token_command` is executed during backend initialization, making single-request unauthenticated local command execution possible on reachable RC deployments without global HTTP authentication. Version 1.73.5 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41179.

title: CVE-2026-41176 - Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage p
description: Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage providers. The RC endpoint `options/set` is exposed without `AuthRequired: true`, but it can mutate global runtime configuration, including the RC option block itself. Starting in version 1.45.0 and prior to version 1.73.5, an unauthenticated attacker can set `rc.NoAuth=true`, which disables the authorization gate for many RC methods registered with `AuthRequired: true` on reachable RC servers that are started without global HTTP authentication. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive administrative functionality, including configuration and operational RC methods. Version 1.73.5 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41176.
